questions.question: Why is React knowledge a prerequisite for Next.js?
options: A) Because Next.js apps are built on React component and rendering concepts
B) Because Next.js replaces JavaScript entirely
C) Because React is only used for databases
D) Because Next.js does not support components
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because Next.js apps are built on React component and rendering concepts

questions.question: What is the benefit of file-based routing in frameworks like Next.js?
options: A) It maps project structure to routes in a predictable way
B) It removes the need for components
C) It only helps CSS organization
D) It replaces authentication
questions.correctAnswer: A) It maps project structure to routes in a predictable way

questions.question: Why does Next.js distinguish between server and client behavior?
options: A) Because some logic can run on the server for data and rendering while interactive logic runs in the browser
B) Because browsers can access databases directly
C) Because servers only render CSS
D) Because JavaScript cannot run on the client
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because some logic can run on the server for data and rendering while interactive logic runs in the browser

questions.question: What is the purpose of server-side rendering or pre-rendering?
options: A) To control when HTML is produced and improve delivery for specific app needs
B) To eliminate JavaScript from all apps
C) To replace routing
D) To avoid HTTP requests
questions.correctAnswer: A) To control when HTML is produced and improve delivery for specific app needs

questions.question: Why is data fetching strategy important in Next.js?
options: A) It affects performance, freshness, caching, and user experience
B) It only affects CSS loading
C) It has no effect after deployment
D) It replaces database schema design
questions.correctAnswer: A) It affects performance, freshness, caching, and user experience

questions.question: What problem do layouts solve in a routed application?
options: A) Reusing shared structure and navigation across route segments
B) Replacing API calls
C) Storing secrets
D) Compiling React code
questions.correctAnswer: A) Reusing shared structure and navigation across route segments

questions.question: Why are forms and mutation flows important in app development?
options: A) They support create, update, and user interaction workflows that change data
B) They only matter for static blogs
C) They replace route handlers
D) They remove the need for validation
questions.correctAnswer: A) They support create, update, and user interaction workflows that change data

questions.question: What is validation meant to do in server actions or API handlers?
options: A) Prevent invalid or unsafe input from reaching business logic
B) Improve typography
C) Replace authentication
D) Only speed up image loading
questions.correctAnswer: A) Prevent invalid or unsafe input from reaching business logic

questions.question: Why does authentication matter in a Next.js product app?
options: A) Because protected pages and user-specific actions require identity and access control
B) Because routing works only after login
C) Because it replaces caching
D) Because server rendering is impossible without it
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because protected pages and user-specific actions require identity and access control

questions.question: What is the value of environment variables in a deployed Next.js app?
options: A) They keep environment-specific settings out of hardcoded source
B) They replace components
C) They style layouts
D) They remove build steps
questions.correctAnswer: A) They keep environment-specific settings out of hardcoded source

questions.question: Why is image optimization a common concern in Next.js apps?
options: A) Because media delivery strongly affects performance and user experience
B) Because apps cannot display raw images
C) Because it replaces SEO
D) Because databases require optimized images
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because media delivery strongly affects performance and user experience

questions.question: What does SEO work involve in a web framework?
options: A) Making pages and metadata easier for search engines and link previews to understand
B) Replacing routing
C) Storing passwords securely
D) Only minifying JavaScript
questions.correctAnswer: A) Making pages and metadata easier for search engines and link previews to understand

questions.question: Why does caching strategy need deliberate decisions?
options: A) Because cached results affect freshness, correctness, and performance
B) Because caches automatically solve all backend problems
C) Because caches only matter in local development
D) Because routing disables caches
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because cached results affect freshness, correctness, and performance

questions.question: What is the purpose of route handlers or backend integration in Next.js?
options: A) Handling server-side request logic close to the application
B) Replacing all databases automatically
C) Styling forms
D) Eliminating deployment
questions.correctAnswer: A) Handling server-side request logic close to the application

questions.question: Why are tests useful in a Next.js codebase?
options: A) They help verify critical UI and data flows as features evolve
B) They only matter for CSS
C) They make reviews unnecessary
D) They replace observability
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help verify critical UI and data flows as features evolve

questions.question: Why does deployment knowledge matter for framework-based apps?
options: A) Because runtime behavior, environment config, and hosting choices affect production outcomes
B) Because local development is enough
C) Because deployment replaces monitoring
D) Because apps run the same everywhere without setup
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because runtime behavior, environment config, and hosting choices affect production outcomes

questions.question: What is the purpose of monitoring in production?
options: A) To observe failures, latency, and behavior after real users begin using the app
B) To compile JSX
C) To replace logging
D) To remove the need for authentication
questions.correctAnswer: A) To observe failures, latency, and behavior after real users begin using the app

questions.question: Why is accessibility still important in a Next.js app?
options: A) Because rendering strategy does not replace semantic and usable interface requirements
B) Because only static HTML needs accessibility
C) Because screen readers ignore framework apps
D) Because auth flows disable accessibility
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because rendering strategy does not replace semantic and usable interface requirements

questions.question: What is the role of route-level architecture in larger apps?
options: A) It keeps features organized and makes scaling navigation and ownership easier
B) It removes the need for components
C) It disables caching
D) It only matters for blogs
questions.correctAnswer: A) It keeps features organized and makes scaling navigation and ownership easier

questions.question: What is the strongest way to learn Next.js effectively?
options: A) Build real apps that mix rendering, routing, mutations, auth, and deployment concerns
B) Memorize route filenames only
C) Ignore React fundamentals
D) Focus only on static marketing pages
questions.correctAnswer: A) Build real apps that mix rendering, routing, mutations, auth, and deployment concerns

questions.question: Which framework is built on React?
options: A) Angular
B) Vue
C) Next.js
D) Django
questions.correctAnswer: C) Next.js
